report: Pump discharge pressure dropped from 145 psi to 82 psi within two minutes of startup. Oil is visible around the output shaft seal.
Hydraulic line pressure slowly declined by 12 psi over four hours. No visible fluid was found during inspection.
Pressure reading fell to zero while a redundant mechanical gauge remained stable at 120 psi. Sensor wiring was loose at the connector.
Pump pressure oscillates between 70 and 110 psi and a rattling sound is present near the inlet. Reservoir level is below minimum.
System pressure is 15 psi below the normal range after maintenance. No leaks or abnormal sounds are present.
Pressure dropped 30 psi after a flexible hydraulic line ruptured near the actuator. Fluid was visible on the floor.
Discharge pressure decreased after maintenance. The bypass valve position indicator shows the valve is fully open.
Pump inlet pressure is abnormally low while discharge pressure and motor current fluctuate. The inlet strainer is heavily fouled.
The digital pressure transmitter reads 18 psi below a recently calibrated mechanical gauge across the full operating range.
System pressure fell gradually as the reservoir level approached empty. Pressure recovered after the reservoir was refilled.
Pressure decreased during a scheduled shutdown after the isolation valve was opened to depressurize the line.
Both pressure transmitters reported zero for twelve seconds while flow, motor current, and the mechanical gauge remained normal. The controller logged a communications timeout.
Discharge pressure is low and the motor is not drawing current. The motor controller reports loss of incoming power.
The supervisory display shows low pressure, but the PLC tag and local transmitter both show 125 psi. A recent software deployment changed the display scaling.
Pressure declined rapidly in a chemical transfer line. A toxic-gas monitor alarmed near the flange, and operators reported visible vapor.
Pressure is low, but one gauge indicates 60 psi and another indicates 118 psi. No flow, temperature, valve-position, or inspection data is available.
Pump discharge pressure dropped and casing temperature increased to 96 degrees Celsius. Grinding noise is present at the bearing housing.
Pressure downstream of the control valve is low. The commanded valve position is 80 percent, but the physical position feedback remains at 12 percent.
Pressure fell by 8 psi during a cold-weather startup. It returned to the normal range within three minutes as fluid temperature increased.
Pressure dropped after maintenance. The seal area is dry, the bypass valve reports closed, the reservoir is full, and motor current is normal. No reliable flow measurement is available.
